name: ddd-workshop
description: DDD pipeline with four paired skills covering domain learning, Big Picture EventStorming, Process Modeling + BC Discovery, and an implementation-time protocol that keeps code aligned with the design artifacts. (1) domain-classroom — teaches general industry patterns of an unfamiliar domain in lecture mode grounded in cognitive-science 8 principles. Output: docs/learning-notes/{domain}-classroom.md. (2) bigpicture — 1:1 analytic tool for Big Picture EventStorming. Maps general patterns + client materials into the canonical Big Picture artifact (time-ordered events, phases, actors, external systems, hotspots, pivots). Phase-by-phase analytic accumulation. Domain-event 4-criteria gate. Korean natural-language + Code Identifier dual notation. Output: docs/eventstorming/ folder from the start (index.md + per-phase {NN-phase}/bigpicture.md), reserving a place for later workflow .md to join the same phase folder. (3) process-modeling — picks one phase from bigpicture and unfolds it into end-to-end workflows. Adds Command(blue)/Policy(lilac)/Read Model(green)/Aggregate candidates/alternative paths. One workflow = one .md inside the phase folder. Cross-phase workflows use the owner-phase rule. Bidirectionally updates bigpicture when new hotspots or BC candidates surface. In-place phase-folder migration if bigpicture is in flat .md form. Closing activity: BC Discovery mode — after enough workflows accumulate, clusters Aggregate/Linguistic/Policy/External/ReadModel/Transactional 6 signals into BC candidates, runs a discussion, and persists the decision as an immutable ADR (docs/adr/{NNNN}-bc-split-{domain}.md). Code module mapping and source code authoring are explicitly out of scope. Initial/Update/Cycle/BC-Discovery 4 modes — repeatable after code is written. (4) implementation-protocol — one-time installer (not invocable per use) that persists a design-aligned coding workflow into the public rules file (AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md). Per work unit (issue/ticket/feature): read relevant design docs (bigpicture, process-modeling, ADR) → check if implementation fits the current design scope → in-scope: plan and code / out-of-scope: discuss with user, update design docs first, then code. Never touches Git workflow, issue tracking, PR policy, CI/CD, or testing — slots into existing workflow sections, or creates a new section if none. Conflicting workflow rules surface user choices (replace / partial edit / abort). Track A/B auto-detected. Graceful degradation when some design artifacts are missing. Install/Merge/Update/Conflict 4 modes. Once installed, every coding session auto-follows the protocol via persistent context engineering.
